procedure.description: Anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF), C5-C6, one level
procedure.facility: Pacific Spine Center, San Francisco, CA

denial.reason_text: Requested procedure does not meet medical necessity criteria. Conservative treatment requirements not satisfied. Member has not completed required course of physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks documented) prior to surgical intervention.
denial.reviewer: Dr. Sarah Mills, MD — HealthFirst Utilization Management

appeal_basis: Member completed 8 weeks of physical therapy (June-August 2024) — records enclosed
MRI findings show severe C5-C6 disc herniation with cord compression — neurological urgency
California Insurance Code Section 10169 requires independent medical review for denials based on medical necessity
Treating physician declaration: delay of surgery risks permanent neurological deficit

clinical_record.chief_complaint: Progressive cervical radiculopathy with bilateral hand weakness, 14 months duration

physical_therapy.provider: Bay Area Physical Therapy, San Francisco
physical_therapy.notes: Limited functional improvement. Pain reduced from 8/10 to 6/10. Weakness and paresthesias unchanged.
physical_therapy.discharge_summary: Patient reached plateau. Surgical consultation recommended.

medications.response: Partial — pain reduced, neurological symptoms unchanged

epidural_steroid_injection.response: Minimal — 20% pain reduction, no improvement in motor weakness

mri_cervical_spine.findings: Severe C5-C6 disc herniation with posterior disc-osteophyte complex. Central and right paracentral protrusion causing significant cord compression (cord signal change noted — myelomalacia). Moderate-severe right C6 neural foraminal stenosis. Mild C4-C5 and C6-C7 degenerative changes.
mri_cervical_spine.impression: Cervical myelopathy at C5-C6 with evidence of cord injury. Surgical evaluation strongly recommended.

neurological_exam.findings: Bilateral grip weakness (3+/5 right, 4/5 left). Hoffman sign positive bilaterally. Hyperreflexia bilateral upper extremities. Gait disturbance noted. Dermatomal sensory loss C6 distribution right.
neurological_exam.impression: Cervical myelopathy — progressive. Surgical risk of delay: permanent spinal cord injury.

clinical_record.treating_physician_declaration: Patient Patricia Chen presents with progressive cervical myelopathy secondary to severe C5-C6 disc herniation. MRI demonstrates cord compression with myelomalacia — evidence of existing cord injury. Conservative treatment has been exhausted including 8 weeks PT, 2 ESI, and 6 months pharmacotherapy. Delay of surgical decompression risks permanent neurological deficit and progressive disability. ACDF C5-C6 is medically necessary and time-sensitive.

question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C5-C6 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?
